title: "Embracing the Hijri New Year: A Journey of Reflection and Renewal"
description: The Islamic New Year, or Hijri New Year, marks the beginning of a new lunar year in the Islamic calendar, which is based on the moon's cycles and begins with the Prophet Muhammad's migration from Mecca to Medina in 622 AD. This occasion is a time for Muslims to reflect on personal growth, the past year, and make plans for the future, with some choosing to fast, pray, and read the Quran, although no specific religious rituals are required.
